role: A monolithic React context hook that centralizes all application state, editor bindings, API orchestration, and cross-cutting interaction handlers for the canvas-based IDE interface.
capabilities: Canvas graph load/persist, node wiring, selection, and deletion
Run orchestration with error surfacing and elapsed-time tracking
SSE event-stream consumption, sequence deduplication, and per-kind dispatch
Chat messaging with streaming, threads, and conversation recording
Structured proposal/offer approval, steering, deferral, dismissal, and revive
UI locus indication, address resolution, spotlighting, and journey record/replay
Studio mockup review, corpus browsing, and address-keyed annotation threading
Address help, addressed history, self-change audit, freshness, and version lookup
Settings/persona/mode/model/voice configuration and live fleet registry mirroring
Push-to-talk and hands-free voice input with barge-in/cancel
TTS audio playback scheduling and stream cancellation
Walkthrough/guide step driving and cog-role execution
substance_why: It declares dozens of state hooks and refs, implements concrete API integrations for graph, chat, voice, annotations, settings, and inbox, and wires handlers for canvas, studio, chat, and voice interactions.
interactions_present: Graph run trigger and retry after runError
UI element click-to-indicate with modal-exclusion guard
Chat send with streaming fallback path
Settings open and per-slot config update
Thread open and conversation recording toggle
interactions_missing: Generate-result apply appears to be a display mirror only (no explicit apply wired in the visible code)
Several one-line helpers (`t`, `selected`, repeated `d`) look like stubs or narrow accessors
Form/build helpers (`fieldValue`, `setField`, `revertLast`, `approveApply`) appear partial and late in the file
No visible debouncing on rapid indicate/resolveUiTarget calls
Explicit cleanup/abort for in-flight SSE or non-voice API requests is not visible
performance_risks: Single context hook causes whole consumer-tree re-renders on any state change
No visible memoization of derived selectors or expensive computations
SSE event merging and chat result processing run on every message
Multiple independent polling/fetching functions without request deduplication
Audio context and MediaRecorder lifecycle managed manually across many refs
Large object state snapshots (events, chat, corpus, inbox) copied into React state
reuse_why: The domain vocabulary, API contracts, and interaction rules are the authoritative source of truth, but the monolithic hook form must be decomposed to avoid render churn and coupling.
decomposition_hints: Split canvas/graph concerns: editor bindings, nodes, edges, runs, stuck nodes, fitGraph
Split chat/proposal concerns: messages, streaming, proposals, threads, inbox offers
Split voice/audio concerns: recorders, playback, barge-in, TTS/STT, voice service
Split studio/review concerns: mockups, corpus, annotations, address help, generate
Split locus/history concerns: indicate, resolveUiTarget, journey, history, self-changes, freshness, versions
Split settings/config concerns: models, personas, modes, roles, voice config, autodetect
Split inbox/escalation concerns into a dedicated controller
Split SSE/event dispatch and mergeEvents into a dedicated stream controller
